service:
Amazon Web Services Billing and Cost Management Dashboards is a service that enables you to create, manage, and share dashboards that combine multiple visualizations of your Amazon Web Services cost and usage data. You can combine multiple data sources including Cost Explorer, Savings Plans, and Reserved Instance metrics into unified dashboards, helping you analyze spending patterns and share cost insights across your organization.

You can use the Amazon Web Services Billing and Cost Management Dashboards API to programmatically create, manage, and share dashboards. This includes creating custom dashboards, configuring widgets, managing dashboard permissions, and sharing dashboards across accounts in your organization.

operations.CreateDashboard: Creates a new dashboard that can contain multiple widgets displaying cost and usage data. You can add custom widgets or use predefined widgets, arranging them in your preferred layout.
operations.CreateScheduledReport: Creates a new scheduled report for a dashboard. A scheduled report automatically generates and delivers dashboard snapshots on a recurring schedule. Reports are delivered within 15 minutes of the scheduled delivery time.
operations.DeleteDashboard: Deletes a specified dashboard. This action cannot be undone.
operations.DeleteScheduledReport: Deletes a specified scheduled report. This is an irreversible operation.
operations.ExecuteScheduledReport:
Triggers an immediate execution of a scheduled report, outside of its regular schedule. The scheduled report must be in ENABLED state. Calling this operation on a DISABLED scheduled report returns a ValidationException.

If a clientToken is provided, the service uses it for idempotency. Requests with the same client token will not trigger a new execution within the same minute.
operations.GetDashboard: Retrieves the configuration and metadata of a specified dashboard, including its widgets and layout settings.
operations.GetResourcePolicy: Retrieves the resource-based policy attached to a dashboard, showing sharing configurations and permissions.
operations.GetScheduledReport: Retrieves the configuration and metadata of a specified scheduled report.
operations.ListDashboards: Returns a list of all dashboards in your account.
operations.ListScheduledReports: Returns a list of scheduled reports in your account.
operations.ListTagsForResource: Returns a list of all tags associated with a specified dashboard resource.
operations.TagResource: Adds or updates tags for a specified dashboard resource.
operations.UntagResource: Removes specified tags from a dashboard resource.
operations.UpdateDashboard: Updates an existing dashboard's properties, including its name, description, and widget configurations.
operations.UpdateScheduledReport: Updates an existing scheduled report's properties, including its name, description, schedule configuration, and widget settings. Only the parameters included in the request are updated; all other properties remain unchanged.

AccessDeniedException.base: You do not have sufficient permissions to perform this action. Verify your IAM permissions and any resource policies.

refs.ExecuteScheduledReportRequest$dryRun: When set to true, validates the scheduled report configuration without triggering an actual execution.
refs.ExecuteScheduledReportResponse$execution: Indicates whether the execution was successfully triggered.
refs.UpdateScheduledReportRequest$clearWidget: Set to true to clear existing widgetIds.
Set to true to clear existing widgetDateRangeOverride.

refs.CreateScheduledReportRequest$clientToken: A unique, case-sensitive identifier that you provide to ensure the idempotency of the request.
refs.ExecuteScheduledReportRequest$clientToke: A unique, case-sensitive identifier that you provide to ensure the idempotency of the request.

ConflictException.base: The request could not be completed due to a conflict with the current state of the resource. For example, attempting to create a resource that already exists or is being created.

CostAndUsageQuery.base: Defines the parameters for retrieving Amazon Web Services cost and usage data. Includes specifications for metrics, time periods, granularity, grouping dimensions, and filtering conditions.

refs.QueryParameters$costAndUsage: The parameters for querying cost and usage data, including metrics, time range, granularity, grouping dimensions, and filters.

CostCategoryValues.base: Specifies the values and match options for cost category-based filtering in cost and usage queries.

refs.Expression$costCategories: The cost category values to include in the filter expression.

refs.CreateDashboardResponse$arn: The ARN of the newly created dashboard.
refs.DeleteDashboardRequest$arn: The ARN of the dashboard to be deleted.
refs.DeleteDashboardResponse$arn: The ARN of the dashboard that was deleted.
refs.GetDashboardRequest$arn: The ARN of the dashboard to retrieve. This is required to uniquely identify the dashboard.
refs.GetResourcePolicyRequest$resourceArn: The ARN of the dashboard whose resource-based policy you want to retrieve.
refs.GetResourcePolicyResponse$resourceArn: The ARN of the dashboard for which the resource-based policy was retrieved.
refs.ScheduledReport$dashboardArn: The ARN of the dashboard associated with the scheduled report.
refs.ScheduledReportInput$dashboardArn: The ARN of the dashboard to generate the scheduled report from.
refs.ScheduledReportSummary$dashboardArn: The ARN of the dashboard associated with the scheduled report.
refs.UpdateDashboardRequest$arn: The ARN of the dashboard to update.
refs.UpdateScheduledReportRequest$dashboardAr: The ARN of the dashboard to associate with the scheduled report.

refs.CreateDashboardRequest$name: The name of the dashboard. The name must be unique within your account.

DashboardReference.base: Contains basic information about a dashboard, including its ARN, name, type, and timestamps.

refs.ListDashboardsResponse$dashboards: An array of dashboard references, containing basic information about each dashboard.

DateTimeRange.base: Defines a time period with explicit start and end times for data queries.

refs.CostAndUsageQuery$timeRange: The time period for which to retrieve data. Can be specified as absolute dates or relative time periods.
refs.ScheduledReport$widgetDateRangeOverride: The date range override applied to widgets in the scheduled report.
refs.ScheduledReportInput$widgetDateRangeOver: The date range override to apply to widgets in the scheduled report.
refs.UpdateScheduledReportRequest$widgetDateR: The date range override to apply to widgets in the scheduled report.

refs.DateTimeValue$type: The type of date/time value: ABSOLUTE for specific dates or RELATIVE for dynamic time periods.

DateTimeValue.base: Represents a point in time that can be specified as either an absolute date (for example, "2025-07-01") or a relative time period using ISO 8601 duration format (for example, "-P3M" for three months ago).

refs.DateTimeRange$startTime: The start time of the date range for querying data.
refs.DateTimeRange$endTime: The end time of the date range for querying data.

refs.CreateDashboardRequest$description: A description of the dashboard's purpose or contents.
refs.ScheduledReport$description: A description of the scheduled report's purpose or contents.
refs.ScheduledReportInput$description: A description of the scheduled report's purpose or contents.
refs.UpdateScheduledReportRequest$description: The new description for the scheduled report.
refs.Widget$description: A description of the widget's purpose or the data it displays.

refs.DimensionValues$key: The key of the dimension to filter on (for example, SERVICE, USAGE_TYPE, or OPERATION).

DimensionValues.base: Specifies the values and match options for dimension-based filtering in cost and usage queries.

refs.Expression$dimensions: The dimension values to include in the filter expression.

DisplayConfig.base: Defines how the widget's data should be visualized, including chart type, color schemes, axis configurations, and other display preferences.

refs.WidgetConfig$displayConfig: The configuration that determines how the retrieved data should be visualized in the widget.

Expression.base: Defines complex filtering conditions using logical operators (AND, OR, NOT) and various filter types.

refs.CostAndUsageQuery$filter: The filter expression to be applied to the cost and usage data.
refs.Expression$not: An expression to negate with NOT logic.

refs.Expression$or: A list of expressions to combine with OR logic.
refs.Expression$and: A list of expressions to combine with AND logic.

refs.GetResourcePolicyResponse$policyDocument: The JSON policy document that represents the dashboard's resource-based policy.
refs.ScheduleConfig$scheduleExpression: The schedule expression that specifies when to trigger the scheduled report run. This value must be a cron expression consisting of six fields separated by white spaces: cron(minutes hours day_of_month month day_of_week year).
refs.ScheduleConfig$scheduleExpressionTimeZon: The time zone for the schedule expression, for example, UTC.
refs.ScheduledReportSummary$scheduleExpressio: The schedule expression that defines when the report runs.
The time zone for the schedule expression, for example, UTC.

refs.DashboardReference$createdAt: The timestamp when the dashboard was created.
refs.DashboardReference$updatedAt: The timestamp when the dashboard was last modified.
refs.GetDashboardResponse$createdAt: The timestamp when the dashboard was created.
refs.GetDashboardResponse$updatedAt: The timestamp when the dashboard was last modified.
refs.HealthStatus$lastRefreshedAt: The timestamp when the health status was last refreshed.
refs.SchedulePeriod$startTime: The start time of the schedule period. If not specified, defaults to the time of the create or update request. The start time cannot be more than 5 minutes before the time of the request.
refs.SchedulePeriod$endTime: The end time of the schedule period. If not specified, defaults to 3 years from the time of the create or update request. The maximum allowed value is 3 years from the current time. Setting an end time beyond this limit returns a ValidationException.
refs.ScheduledReport$createdAt: The timestamp when the scheduled report was created.
refs.ScheduledReport$updatedAt: The timestamp when the scheduled report was last modified.
refs.ScheduledReport$lastExecutionAt: The timestamp of the most recent execution of the scheduled report.

refs.CostAndUsageQuery$granularity: The granularity of the retrieved data: HOURLY, DAILY, or MONTHLY.
refs.ReservationCoverageQuery$granularity: The time granularity of the retrieved data: HOURLY, DAILY, or MONTHLY.
refs.ReservationUtilizationQuery$granularity: The time granularity of the retrieved data: HOURLY, DAILY, or MONTHLY.
refs.SavingsPlansCoverageQuery$granularity: The time granularity of the retrieved data: HOURLY, DAILY, or MONTHLY.
refs.SavingsPlansUtilizationQuery$granularity: The time granularity of the retrieved data: HOURLY, DAILY, or MONTHLY.

GraphDisplayConfig.base: Defines the visual representation settings for widget data, including the visualization type, styling options, and display preferences for different metric types.

refs.DisplayConfig$graph: The configuration for graphical display of the widget data, including chart type and visual options.

GroupDefinition.base: Specifies how to group cost and usage data.

refs.GroupDefinition$key: The key to use for grouping cost and usage data.

refs.CostAndUsageQuery$groupBy: Specifies how to group the retrieved data, such as by SERVICE, ACCOUNT, or TAG.
refs.ReservationCoverageQuery$groupBy: Specifies how to group the Reserved Instance coverage data, such as by service, Region, or instance type.
refs.ReservationUtilizationQuery$groupBy: Specifies how to group the Reserved Instance utilization data, such as by service, Region, or instance type.
refs.SavingsPlansCoverageQuery$groupBy: Specifies how to group the Savings Plans coverage data, such as by service or instance family.

HealthStatus.base: Contains the health status information for a scheduled report, including the status code and any reasons for an unhealthy state.

refs.ExecuteScheduledReportResponse$healthSta: The health status of the scheduled report after the execution request.
refs.ScheduledReport$healthStatus: The health status of the scheduled report at last refresh time.
refs.ScheduledReportSummary$healthStatus: The health status of the scheduled report as of its last refresh time.

refs.HealthStatus$statusCode: The health status code. HEALTHY indicates the scheduled report is configured properly and has all required permissions to execute. UNHEALTHY indicates the scheduled report is unable to deliver the notification to the default Amazon EventBridge EventBus in your account and your action is needed. The reason for the unhealthy state is captured in the health status reasons.

refs.Widget$horizontalOffset: Specifies the starting column position of the widget in the dashboard's grid layout. Used to control widget placement.

InternalServerException.base: An internal error occurred while processing the request. Retry your request. If the problem persists, contact Amazon Web Services Support.

refs.CostCategoryValues$matchOptions: The match options for cost category values, such as EQUALS, CONTAINS, STARTS_WITH, or ENDS_WITH.
refs.DimensionValues$matchOptions: The match options for dimension values, such as EQUALS, CONTAINS, STARTS_WITH, or ENDS_WITH.
refs.TagValues$matchOptions: The match options for tag values, such as EQUALS, CONTAINS, STARTS_WITH, or ENDS_WITH.

refs.ListDashboardsRequest$maxResults: The maximum number of results to return in a single call. The default value is 20.
refs.ListScheduledReportsRequest$maxResults: The maximum number of results to return in a single call. Valid range is 1 to 100. The default value is 50.

refs.CostAndUsageQuery$metrics:
The specific cost and usage metrics to retrieve.

Valid values for CostAndUsageQuery metrics are AmortizedCost, BlendedCost, NetAmortizedCost, NetUnblendedCost, NormalizedUsageAmount, UnblendedCost, and UsageQuantity.
refs.ReservationCoverageQuery$metrics:
The coverage metrics to include in the results.

Valid values for ReservationCoverageQuery metrics are Hour, Unit, and Cost.
refs.SavingsPlansCoverageQuery$metrics:
The coverage metrics to include in the results.

Valid value for SavingsPlansCoverageQuery metrics is SpendCoveredBySavingsPlans.

refs.ListDashboardsRequest$nextToken: The token for the next page of results. Use the value returned in the previous response.
refs.ListDashboardsResponse$nextToken: The token to use to retrieve the next page of results. Not returned if there are no more results to retrieve.
refs.ListScheduledReportsRequest$nextToken: The token for the next page of results. Use the value returned in the previous response.
refs.ListScheduledReportsResponse$nextToken: The token to use to retrieve the next page of results. Not returned if there are no more results to retrieve.

QueryParameters.base: Defines the data retrieval parameters for a widget.

refs.WidgetConfig$queryParameters: The parameters that define what data the widget should retrieve and how it should be filtered or grouped.

ReservationCoverageQuery.base: Defines the parameters for querying Reserved Instance coverage data, including grouping options, metrics, and sorting preferences.

refs.QueryParameters$reservationCoverage: The parameters for querying Reserved Instance coverage data, showing how much of your eligible instance usage is covered by Reserved Instances.

ReservationUtilizationQuery.base: Defines the parameters for querying Reserved Instance utilization data, including grouping options and time granularity.

refs.QueryParameters$reservationUtilization: The parameters for querying Reserved Instance utilization data, showing how effectively your Reserved Instances are being used.

ResourceNotFoundException.base: The specified resource (dashboard, policy, or widget) was not found. Verify the ARN and try again.

ResourceTag.base: A key-value pair that can be attached to a dashboard for organization and management purposes.

refs.ResourceTag$key: The key of the tag to be attached to the dashboard resource.

refs.UntagResourceRequest$resourceTagKeys: The keys of the tags to remove from the dashboard resource.

refs.CreateDashboardRequest$resourceTags: The tags to apply to the dashboard resource for organization and management.
refs.CreateScheduledReportRequest$resourceTag: The tags to apply to the scheduled report resource for organization and management.
refs.ListTagsForResourceResponse$resourceTags: The list of tags associated with the specified dashboard resource.
refs.TagResourceRequest$resourceTags: The tags to add to the dashboard resource.

refs.ResourceTag$value: The value of the tag to be attached to the dashboard resource.

SavingsPlansCoverageQuery.base: Defines the parameters for querying Savings Plans coverage data, including metrics, grouping options, and time granularity.

refs.QueryParameters$savingsPlansCoverage: The parameters for querying Savings Plans coverage data, showing how much of your eligible compute usage is covered by Savings Plans.

SavingsPlansUtilizationQuery.base: Defines the parameters for querying Savings Plans utilization data, including time granularity and sorting preferences.

refs.QueryParameters$savingsPlansUtilization: The parameters for querying Savings Plans utilization data, showing how effectively your Savings Plans are being used.

ScheduleConfig.base: Defines the schedule for a scheduled report, including the cron expression, time zone, active period, and the schedule state.

refs.ScheduledReport$scheduleConfig: The schedule configuration that defines when and how often the report is generated.
refs.ScheduledReportInput$scheduleConfig: The schedule configuration that defines when and how often the report is generated. If the schedule state is not specified, it defaults to ENABLED.
refs.UpdateScheduledReportRequest$scheduleCon: The updated schedule configuration for the report.

SchedulePeriod.base: Defines the active time period for execution of the scheduled report.

refs.ScheduleConfig$schedulePeriod: The time period during which the schedule is active.

refs.ScheduleConfig$state: The state of the schedule. ENABLED means the scheduled report runs according to its schedule expression. DISABLED means the scheduled report is paused and will not run until re-enabled.

ScheduledReport.base: Contains the full configuration and metadata of a scheduled report.

refs.CreateScheduledReportResponse$arn: The ARN of the newly created scheduled report.
refs.DeleteScheduledReportRequest$arn: The ARN of the scheduled report to delete.
refs.DeleteScheduledReportResponse$arn: The ARN of the scheduled report that was deleted.
refs.ExecuteScheduledReportRequest$arn: The ARN of the scheduled report to execute.
refs.GetScheduledReportRequest$arn: The ARN of the scheduled report to retrieve.
refs.UpdateScheduledReportRequest$arn: The ARN of the scheduled report to update.
refs.UpdateScheduledReportResponse$arn: The ARN of the updated scheduled report.

ScheduledReportInput.base: Defines the configuration for creating a new scheduled report, including the dashboard, schedule, execution role, and optional widget settings.

refs.CreateScheduledReportRequest$scheduledRe: The configuration for the scheduled report, including the dashboard to report on, the schedule, and the execution role that the service will use to generate the dashboard snapshot.

refs.UpdateScheduledReportRequest$name: The new name for the scheduled report.

ScheduledReportSummary.base: Contains summary information for a scheduled report.

refs.ListScheduledReportsResponse$scheduledRe: An array of scheduled report summaries, containing basic information about each scheduled report.

ServiceQuotaExceededException.base: The request would exceed a service quota. Review the service quotas for Amazon Web Services Billing and Cost Management Dashboards and retry your request.

refs.ScheduledReport$scheduledReportExecution: The ARN of the IAM role that the scheduled report uses to execute. Amazon Web Services Billing and Cost Management Dashboards will assume this IAM role while executing the scheduled report.
refs.ScheduledReportInput$scheduledReportExec: The ARN of the IAM role that the scheduled report uses to execute. Amazon Web Services Billing and Cost Management Dashboards will assume this IAM role while executing the scheduled report.
refs.UpdateScheduledReportRequest$scheduledRe: The ARN of the IAM role that the scheduled report uses to execute. Amazon Web Services Billing and Cost Management Dashboards will assume this IAM role while executing the scheduled report.

refs.HealthStatus$statusReasons: The list of reasons for the current health status. Only present when the status is UNHEALTHY.

refs.CostCategoryValues$key: The key of the cost category to filter on.
refs.TagValues$key: The key of the tag to filter on.

refs.CostCategoryValues$values: The values to match for the specified cost category key.
refs.DimensionValues$values: The values to match for the specified dimension key.
refs.TagValues$values: The values to match for the specified tag key.

TableDisplayConfigStruct.base: Configuration structure for customizing the tabular display of widget data.

refs.DisplayConfig$table: The configuration for tabular display of the widget data.

TagValues.base: Specifies tag-based filtering options for cost and usage queries.

refs.Expression$tags: The tag values to include in the filter expression.

ThrottlingException.base: The request was denied due to request throttling. Reduce the frequency of requests and use exponential backoff.

ValidationException.base: The input parameters do not satisfy the requirements. Check the error message for specific validation details.

refs.GraphDisplayConfig$visualType: The type of visualization to use for the data.

Widget.base: A configurable visualization component within a dashboard that displays specific cost and usage metrics. Each widget can show data as charts or tables and includes settings for data querying, filtering, and visual presentation.

WidgetConfig.base: Defines the complete configuration for a widget, including data retrieval settings and visualization preferences.

refs.Widget$configs: An array of configurations that define the data queries and display settings for the widget.

refs.Widget$height: The height of the widget in row spans. The dashboard layout consists of a grid system.

refs.ScheduledReport$widgetIds: The list of widget identifiers included in the scheduled report.
refs.ScheduledReportInput$widgetIds: The list of widget identifiers to include in the scheduled report. If not specified, all widgets in the dashboard are included.
refs.ScheduledReportSummary$widgetIds: The list of widget identifiers included in the scheduled report.
refs.UpdateScheduledReportRequest$widgetIds: The list of widget identifiers to include in the scheduled report. If not specified, all widgets in the dashboard are included.

refs.CreateDashboardRequest$widgets: An array of widget configurations that define the visualizations to be displayed in the dashboard. Each dashboard can contain up to 20 widgets.
refs.GetDashboardResponse$widgets: An array of widget configurations that make up the dashboard.
refs.UpdateDashboardRequest$widgets: The updated array of widget configurations for the dashboard. Replaces all existing widgets.

refs.Widget$width: The width of the widget in column spans. The dashboard layout consists of a grid system.
